title: identity — driver specifications
description: Two driver contracts. MfaSecretEncryptor drivers wrap the KMS envelope encryption for Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted. CredentialHasher drivers wrap the password-hash algorithm. Every driver is resolved via `TranslatorDriverManager`-style factory in IdentityServiceProvider.

methods.returns: Ciphertext blob (encrypted data-key + AES-256-GCM ciphertext + IV + auth tag, base64-encoded). Ready to persist to Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted.
Plaintext TOTP secret (base32). Caller is expected to consume it in a bounded scope + zero it out.
Ciphertext re-encrypted under the current KMS data key. Idempotent — returns the input when already under the current key.
Argon2id/bcrypt hash string (self-describing — the algorithm identifier is embedded in the hash prefix).
Constant-time comparison result. Constant-time property enforced at implementation.
True when the hash was produced under an older cost parameter than the current config. Callers rehash on next successful verify.

mfa_secret_encryptor_drivers.name: kms
strengths: Managed key rotation via KMS built-in rotation policy
Auditable — every decrypt call appears in CloudTrail
weaknesses: Requires AWS credentials + KMS endpoint reachable

mfa_secret_encryptor_drivers.name: local
mfa_secret_encryptor_drivers.purpose: DEV-ONLY. Reads a key from disk and performs AES-256-GCM inline. Refuses to load when APP_ENV=production. Retained here for local-development ergonomics.

credential_hasher_drivers.name: argon2id
strengths: Winner of the Password Hashing Competition
weaknesses: Higher CPU + RAM per hash (default 64 MiB) than bcrypt
Requires libsodium in the PHP runtime

credential_hasher_drivers.name: bcrypt
strengths: Ships with every PHP install (no extension required)
weaknesses: Not memory-hard — GPU-friendly compared to argon2id
